title: dactylis glomerata var. sibthorpii
intro: Dactylis glomerata var. sibthorpii, also known as Sibthorp's grass or orchard grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata var. sibthorpii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
